member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorSparseValues
member.summary: Sparse values property of a glTF

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorSparseValues.bufferView
member.summary: The index of the bufferView with sparse values. Referenced bufferView can't have ARRAY_BUFFER or ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER target.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorSparseValues.byteOffset
member.summary: The offset relative to the start of the bufferView in bytes. Must be aligned.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase
member.summary: An accessor defines a method for retrieving data as typed arrays from within a buffer view. See . accessor in the glTF 2.0 specification.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.bufferView
member.summary: The index of the bufferView. If this is undefined, look in the sparse object for the index and value buffer views.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.byteOffset
member.summary: The offset relative to the start of the bufferView in bytes. This must be a multiple of the size of the component datatype.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.componentType
member.summary: The datatype of components in the attribute. All valid values correspond to WebGL enums. The corresponding typed arrays are: `Int8Array`, `Uint8Array`, `Int16Array`, `Uint16Array`, `Uint32Array`, and `Float32Array`, respectively. 5125 (UNSIGNED_INT) is only allowed when the accessor contains indices i.e., the accessor is only referenced by `primitive.indices`.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.normalized
member.summary: Specifies whether integer data values should be normalized (`true`) to [0, 1] (for unsigned types) or [-1, 1] (for signed types), or converted directly (`false`) when they are accessed. Must be `false` when accessor is used for animation data.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.count
member.summary: The number of attributes referenced by this accessor, not to be confused with the number of bytes or number of components.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.type
member.summary: Specifies if the attribute is a scalar, vector, or matrix, and the number of elements in the vector or matrix.

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.GetAttributeType
member.summary: typed/cached getter from the string.
member.returns: The Accessor's attribute type, if it could be retrieved correctly. otherwise

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.SetAttributeType(GLTFast.Schema.GltfAccessorAttributeType)
member.summary: typed setter for the string.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.max
member.summary:
Maximum value of each component in this attribute. Both min and max arrays have the same length. The length is determined by the value of the type property; it can be 1, 2, 3, 4, 9, or 16.
When `componentType` is `5126` (FLOAT) each array value must be stored as double-precision JSON number with numerical value which is equal to buffer-stored single-precision value to avoid extra runtime conversions.
`normalized` property has no effect on array values: they always correspond to the actual values stored in the buffer. When accessor is sparse, this property must contain max values of accessor data with sparse substitution applied.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AccessorBase.min
member.summary:
Minimum value of each component in this attribute. Both min and max arrays have the same length. The length is determined by the value of the type property; it can be 1, 2, 3, 4, 9, or 16.
When `componentType` is `5126` (FLOAT) each array value must be stored as double-precision JSON number with numerical value which is equal to buffer-stored single-precision value to avoid extra runtime conversions.
`normalized` property has no effect on array values: they always correspond to the actual values stored in the buffer. When accessor is sparse, this property must contain min values of accessor data with sparse substitution applied.

member.summary: Sparse storage of attributes that deviate from their initialization value.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.SpotLight
member.summary: glTF spot light properties

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.SpotLight.innerConeAngle
member.summary: Angle, in radians, from centre of spotlight where falloff begins Must be greater than or equal to 0 and less than outerConeAngle

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.SpotLight.outerConeAngle
member.summary: Angle, in radians, from centre of spotlight where falloff ends. Must be greater than innerConeAngle and less than or equal to PI / 2.0.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AnimationSampler.input
member.summary: The index of an accessor containing keyframe input values, e.G., time. That accessor must have componentType `FLOAT`. The values represent time in seconds with `time[0] >= 0.0`, and strictly increasing values, i.e., `time[n + 1] > time[n]`

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AnimationSampler.interpolation
member.summary: Interpolation algorithm. When an animation targets a node's rotation, and the animation's interpolation is `\"LINEAR\"`, spherical linear interpolation (slerp) should be used to interpolate quaternions. When interpolation is `\"STEP\"`, animated value remains constant to the value of the first point of the timeframe, until the next timeframe.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.AnimationSampler.output
member.summary: The index of an accessor, containing keyframe output values. Output and input accessors must have the same `count`. When sampler is used with TRS target, output accessor's componentType must be `FLOAT`.

member.summary: Reference to a texture.

member.summary: The index of the texture.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.TextureInfoBase.texCoord
member.summary: This integer value is used to construct a string in the format TEXCOORD_<set index> which is a reference to a key in mesh.primitives.attributes (e.g. A value of 0 corresponds to TEXCOORD_0).

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.TextureInfoBase.SetTextureTransform(GLTFast.Schema.TextureTransform)
member.summary: Applies a texture transform by initializing (if required) and setting its field.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.NormalTextureInfoBase
member.summary: Normal map specific texture info

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.NormalTextureInfoBase.scale
member.summary: The scalar multiplier applied to each normal vector of the texture. This value is ignored if normalTexture is not specified. This value is linear.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase
member.summary: A set of parameter values that are used to define the metallic-roughness material model from Physically-Based Rendering (PBR) methodology.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.baseColorFactor
member.summary: The RGBA components of the base color of the material. The fourth component (A) is the opacity of the material. These values are linear.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.BaseColor
member.summary: Base color of the material in linear color space.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.BaseColorTexture
member.summary: The base color texture. This texture contains RGB(A) components in sRGB color space. The first three components (RGB) specify the base color of the material. If the fourth component (A) is present, it represents the opacity of the material. Otherwise, an opacity of 1.0 is assumed.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.metallicFactor
member.summary: The metalness of the material. A value of 1.0 means the material is a metal. A value of 0.0 means the material is a dielectric. Values in between are for blending between metals and dielectrics such as dirty metallic surfaces. This value is linear.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.roughnessFactor
member.summary: The roughness of the material. A value of 1.0 means the material is completely rough. A value of 0.0 means the material is completely smooth. This value is linear.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.PbrMetallicRoughnessBase.MetallicRoughnessTexture
member.summary: The metallic-roughness texture has two components. The first component (R) contains the metallic-ness of the material. The second component (G) contains the roughness of the material. These values are linear. If the third component (B) and/or the fourth component (A) are present, they are ignored.

member.summary: The sheen color red, green and blue components in linear space.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase
member.summary: The material appearance of a primitive.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.AlphaMode
member.summary: The material’s alpha rendering mode enumeration specifying the interpretation of the alpha value of the base color.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.AlphaMode.Opaque
member.summary: The alpha value is ignored, and the rendered output is fully opaque.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.AlphaMode.Mask
member.summary: The rendered output is either fully opaque or fully transparent depending on the alpha value and the specified alphaCutoff value

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.AlphaMode.Blend
member.summary: The alpha value is used to composite the source and destination areas. The rendered output is combined with the background using the normal painting operation.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.Extensions
member.summary: Material extensions.

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.UnsetExtensions
member.summary: Sets to null.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.PbrMetallicRoughness
member.summary: A set of parameter values that are used to define the metallic-roughness material model from Physically-Based Rendering (PBR) methodology.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.NormalTexture
member.summary: A tangent space normal map. Each texel represents the XYZ components of a normal vector in tangent space.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.OcclusionTexture
member.summary: The occlusion map is a greyscale texture, with white indicating areas that should receive full indirect lighting and black indicating no indirect lighting.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.EmissiveTexture
member.summary: The emissive map controls the color and intensity of the light being emitted by the material. This texture contains RGB components in sRGB color space. If a fourth component (A) is present, it is ignored.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.emissiveFactor
member.summary: The RGB components of the emissive color of the material. If an emissiveTexture is specified, this value is multiplied with the texel values.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.Emissive
member.summary: Emissive color of the material.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.alphaMode
member.summary: The material's alpha rendering mode enumeration specifying the interpretation of the alpha value of the main factor and texture. In `OPAQUE` mode, the alpha value is ignored and the rendered output is fully opaque. In `MASK` mode, the rendered output is either fully opaque or fully transparent depending on the alpha value and the specified alpha cutoff value. In `BLEND` mode, the alpha value is used to composite the source and destination areas. The rendered output is combined with the background using the normal painting operation (i.e. the Porter and Duff over operator).

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.GetAlphaMode
member.summary: typed and cached getter for string.
member.returns: Alpha mode if it was retrieved correctly. otherwise

member.name: M:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.SetAlphaMode(GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.AlphaMode)
member.summary: typed setter for string.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.alphaCutoff
member.summary: Specifies the cutoff threshold when in `MASK` mode. If the alpha value is greater than or equal to this value then it is rendered as fully opaque, otherwise, it is rendered as fully transparent. This value is ignored for other modes.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.doubleSided
member.summary: Specifies whether the material is double sided. When this value is false, back-face culling is enabled. When this value is true, back-face culling is disabled and double sided lighting is enabled. The back-face must have its normals reversed before the lighting equation is evaluated.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.RequiresNormals
member.summary: True if the material requires the mesh to have normals.

member.name: P:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialBase.RequiresTangents
member.summary: True if the material requires the mesh to have tangents.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.NodeBase
member.summary: An object defining the hierarchy relations and the local transform of its content.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.NodeBase.children
member.summary: The indices of this node's children.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.NodeBase.mesh
member.summary: The index of the mesh in this node.

member.summary: A scalar multiplier controlling the amount of occlusion applied. A value of 0.0 means no occlusion. A value of 1.0 means full occlusion. This value is ignored if the corresponding texture is not specified. This value is linear.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase
member.summary: Geometry to be rendered with the given material.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase.attributes
member.summary: A dictionary object, where each key corresponds to mesh attribute semantic and each value is the index of the accessor containing attribute's data.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase.indices
member.summary: The index of the accessor that contains mesh indices. When this is not defined, the primitives should be rendered without indices using `drawArrays()`. When defined, the accessor must contain indices: the `bufferView` referenced by the accessor must have a `target` equal to 34963 (ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER); a `byteStride` that is tightly packed, i.e., 0 or the byte size of `componentType` in bytes; `componentType` must be 5121 (UNSIGNED_BYTE), 5123 (UNSIGNED_SHORT) or 5125 (UNSIGNED_INT), the latter is only allowed when `OES_element_index_uint` extension is used; `type` must be `\"SCALAR\"`.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase.material
member.summary: The index of the material to apply to this primitive when rendering.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase.mode
member.summary: The type of primitives to render. All valid values correspond to WebGL enums.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MeshPrimitiveBase.targets
member.summary: An array of Morph Targets, each Morph Target is a dictionary mapping attributes to their deviations in the Morph Target (index of the accessor containing the attribute displacements' data).

member.summary: ` Sets to null.

member.name: T:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialIor
member.summary: The dielectric BRDF of the metallic-roughness material in glTF uses a fixed value of 1.5 for the index of refraction. This is a good fit for many plastics and glass, but not for other materials like water or asphalt, sapphire or diamond. This extension allows users to set the index of refraction to a certain value.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialIor.defaultIndexOfRefraction
member.summary: Default index of refraction. A good compromise for most opaque, dielectric materials.

member.name: F:GLTFast.Schema.MaterialIor.ior
member.summary: The index of refraction.

member.summary: The offset of the UV coordinate origin as a factor of the texture dimensions.

member.summary: Rotate the UVs by this many radians counter-clockwise around the origin. This is equivalent to a similar rotation of the image clockwise.

member.summary: The scale factor applied to the components of the UV coordinates.

member.name: T:GLTFast.SubMeshAssignment
member.summary: Usually one glTF primitive relates to one Unity sub-mesh. Sometimes the primitives of one mesh share the same vertex buffer accessors. To avoid duplicate import of those vertex buffers this struct reassigns the vertex buffer of one primitive (at VertexBufferIndex) to another (Primitive).

member.name: M:GLTFast.GltfImportBase.CreateUniqueNames
member.summary: glTF nodes have no requirement to be named or have specific names. Some Unity systems like animation and importers require unique names for Nodes with the same parent. For each node this method creates names that are: - Not empty - Unique amongst nodes with identical parent node
member.returns: Array containing each node's parent node index (or -1 for root nodes)

member.name: T:GLTFast.IDeferAgent
member.summary: An IDeferAgent decides whether to interrupt a preempt-able procedure running on the main thread at the current point in time. This decision manages the trade-off between minimum procedure duration and a responsive frame rate.

member.name: M:GLTFast.IDeferAgent.ShouldDefer
member.summary: This will be called at various points in the loading procedure.
member.returns: True if the remaining work of the loading procedure should be deferred to the next frame/Update loop invocation. False if work can continue.

member.name: M:GLTFast.IDeferAgent.ShouldDefer(System.Single)
member.summary: Indicates if upcoming work should be deferred to the next frame.
member.param: Predicted duration of upcoming processing in seconds
member.returns: True if the remaining work of the loading procedure should be deferred to the next frame/Update loop invocation. False if work can continue.

member.name: M:GLTFast.IDeferAgent.BreakPoint
member.summary: Conditional yield. May continue right away or yield once, based on time.
member.returns: If returns true, returns Task.Yield(). Otherwise returns sync

member.name: M:GLTFast.IDeferAgent.BreakPoint(System.Single)
member.summary: Conditional yield. May continue right away or yield once, based on time and duration.
member.param: Predicted duration of upcoming processing in seconds
member.returns: If returns true, returns Task.Yield(). Otherwise returns sync

member.name: T:GLTFast.PrimitivesComparer
member.summary: This is similar to , except it does not take the indices into account. That's useful to detect meshes that share large vertex buffers, but have different indices, which is inefficient (in Unity) and discouraged.

member.summary: Use original node names. Fallback to mesh's name (if present) Fallback to "Node_<index>" as last resort.

member.name: F:GLTFast.NameImportMethod.OriginalUnique
member.summary: Identical to Original, but names are made unique (within their hierarchical position) by supplementing a continuous number. This is required for correct animation target lookup and import continuity.

member.name: T:GLTFast.MeshResult
member.summary: This struct holds the result of a glTF to Unity mesh conversion. During an import, glTF meshes (composed of primitives) will be converted to Unity Meshes (with sub-meshes). glTF meshes and Unity Meshes do not necessarily relate one-to-one. One glTF mesh (with multiple primitives) might be converted to multiple Unity Meshes (e.g. because of incompatible vertex buffer structure).

member.summary: Original glTF mesh index

member.summary: Original glTF mesh primitive index per sub-mesh

member.summary: glTF material index per sub-mesh

member.name: F:GLTFast.MeshResult.mesh
member.summary: Converted Unity Mesh

member.param: Original glTF mesh primitive index per sub-mesh

Index of the parent's node. If it's null, the node's a root-level node

member.name: M:GLTFast.IInstantiator.SetNodeName(System.UInt32,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the name of a node. If a node has no name it falls back to the first valid mesh name. Null otherwise.
member.param: Index of the node to be named.

member.name: M:GLTFast.IInstantiator.AddPrimitive(System.UInt32,System.String,GLTFast.MeshResult,System.UInt32[],System.Nullable{System.UInt32},System.Single[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a Mesh/MeshResult to a Node for rendering purpose.
member.param: If a skin was attached, the joint indices. Null otherwise
Root joint node index, if present
Per glTF mesh numeration. A glTF mesh is converted into one or more MeshResults which are numbered consecutively. is called once for each of those MeshResults.

member.summary: Adds a Mesh/MeshResult to a Node with the purpose of rendering multiple instances of this MeshResult and material combination (through glTF extension EXT_mesh_gpu_instancing). In contrast to it lacks joints/skin support.
member.param: Per glTF mesh numeration. A glTF mesh is converted into one or more MeshResults which are numbered consecutively. is called once for each of those MeshResults.

member.name: M:GLTFast.IInstantiator.EndScene(System.UInt32[])
member.summary: Is called at last, after all scene content has been created. Immediately afterwards the scene will be rendered, so use it to finally prepare the instance.
member.param: Indices of root level nodes in scene
